_provenance.stage: Stage 4 — Alternative Framing (DeepSeek/ChatGPT, browser, on v3)
_provenance.note: Stage 4 is retired per CLAUDE.md but was run for completeness. Highest-value adds for Stage 6 synthesis: (1) Anwar Ibrahim pledged in 2018 AND 2023 to abolish/reform the Sedition Act — the CURRENT PM has personally pledged what is still not delivered. This materially strengthens the editorial 'gap' beyond a 14-year Najib promise. (2) SUARAM 2025 finding: 'half of all cases under the Sedition Act involved criticism of political leaders, with the government building a narrative that dissent is an act of rudeness' — strong rights-card add. (3) MCMC's parallel infrastructure: 15,296 fake royal accounts (Jan-Apr 2026) and takedown requests rising 19,344 → 42,027 for offensive content — administrative-enforcement context. (4) Home Minister stated NO surge in Sedition Act cases since 2015 (Feb 2025) — government's counter-narrative. Other Stage 4 adds (East Malaysia, B40 class, gender) are valid but tangential to this specific story's core gap; will be noted but not added as new cards unless they substantively change the editorial.

highest_value_adds.fact: Anwar Ibrahim pledged in 2018 AND in 2023 to abolish or reform the Sedition Act. Amnesty noted in Oct 2025 that these were broken pledges.
highest_value_adds.t4a_decision: INCORPORATE into reframe or view in Stage 6 — current PM's personally-broken pledge is stronger than Najib's 14-year-old one.

highest_value_adds.fact: SUARAM Human Rights Report 2025: 'half of all cases under the Sedition Act involved criticism of political leaders, with the government building a narrative that dissent is an act of rudeness.'
highest_value_adds.t4a_decision: INCORPORATE into card 2 (Rights) or hook sub. Materially changes the framing from '3R protection' to 'dissent suppression'.

highest_value_adds.fact: MCMC identified 15,296 fake royal accounts (26 royals) Jan-Apr 2026, of which 14,750 were taken down.
highest_value_adds.t4a_decision: POSSIBLY incorporate as a context note — shows real online provocations existed before Zahid's call, addresses Stage 2c's omission flag.

highest_value_adds.fact: MCMC takedown requests for offensive content rose 19,344 in 2024 → 42,027 in 2025 (more than doubled).
highest_value_adds.t4a_decision: POSSIBLY incorporate — administrative enforcement is also rising sharply.

highest_value_adds.fact: Home Minister Saifuddin Nasution stated in February 2025: NO surge in Sedition Act cases since 2015 — directly contradicting CIJ/SUARAM characterisation.
highest_value_adds.t4a_decision: POSSIBLY incorporate as government counter-position in source_notes; helps balance but risks weakening the editorial.

highest_value_adds.fact: Deputy Home Minister: 90 arrests under Sedition Act 2024-2025 (58 in 2025, 32 in 2024) per Home Ministry — different methodology from CIJ's 36 'monitored cases'.
highest_value_adds.t4a_decision: NOTE in source_notes — methodological discrepancy strengthens the claim that enforcement is rising (Home Ministry's own number is HIGHER than CIJ's monitored-incidents number).

valid_but_tangential: East Malaysia perspective (Mukmin Nantang Bajau Laut case; UMS students) — true and important but not core to this specific 'firmer law on insults' story arc; could justify a follow-up issue.
B40 class dimension (RM500K fine ≈ 20 years median household income) — valid framing but adds complexity; consider for synthesis_notes only.
Gender pattern (women prosecuted for royalty-adjacent TikTok speech) — valid; consider for follow-up issue.

Singapore MRHA / South Africa PEPUDA / Thailand Article 112 / India 295A / Turkey Article 299 international comparisons — too many for a 7-card issue, but the 'mediation over punishment' framing in the reframe already gestures at Singapore.

international_parallels_verified_by_stag.Singapore_MRHA: https://sso.agc.gov.sg/Act/MRHA1990 — civil restraining orders, not criminal prosecution
international_parallels_verified_by_stag.South_Africa_PEPUDA: https://www.sahrc.org.za/ — civil remedies via Equality Courts
international_parallels_verified_by_stag.Thailand_Article_112: 3-15 years imprisonment for defamation of royalty

overall_assessment: Analysis is legally precise on the Zahid call vs existing landscape but incomplete: missing East Malaysia, missing class dimension, missing gender, missing Anwar's broken pledges, missing SUARAM's 'half of cases target political critics' finding. Stage 4's most actionable adds: incorporate Anwar's broken pledges and SUARAM's political-critic finding into Stage 6 synthesis.
